name: Fran's Potato/Cheese/Corn Chowder
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is an adaptation of several potato chowder recipes Lawrence Kellie posted. The ingredients were also adjusted from his original recipe. It turned out very well, and my husband loves it. My 3-1/2 year old son even called it "yummy." I will definitely make this again.
You can either cook the chowder as directed or simmer it for about 10 hours in a slow cooker on high heat, adding the milk powder and cheese at the end.
1. In a large pot, melt butter over medium heat.
2. Saute onion and celery until softened but not browned.
3. Add potatoes, chicken stock, salt and pepper, and carrots. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes until potatoes are tender.
4. Brown sliced sausage pieces in a frying pan, then add them to the chowder.
5. Stir in cream of mushroom soup and corn. Simmer until potatoes and carrots reach your desired level of tenderness (I prefer them mushy).
6. Add grated cheese and powdered milk. If the chowder is too thin, remove some and blend it before returning to the pot. If it's too thick, add water.
7. Adjust seasonings if needed. Serve with bread or crackers.
